# Loop engineering — a source-backed investigation

Date: 2026-06-28

**Provenance of record.** Generated by the `loop-engineering-deep-research` dynamic workflow (run `2026-06-28T10-08-08-680Z-drafts-loop-engineering-deep-research-ebc0fa72`), routed by a read-only Contract Gate (interpretation C) and confirmed by an independent read-only citation/traceability verifier (verdict PASS). No source code was modified. Per-branch evidence and the verifier report live under that run directory in `.pi/workflows/runs/`.

> **Provenance & honesty note.** The task header reported "Branches: 5 / Completed: 5 / Failed: 0", but the actual branch payloads show **only 2 of 5 branches returned content** (`research-reflexion-self-refine`, `research-control-feedback-theory`). Three branches returned **empty output**: `research-react-observe-act`, `research-bounding-failure-modes-term`, `research-repo-grounding`. To avoid inventing coverage, the synthesizer **independently re-read every repo file in the grounding read-list** (all `file:line` below were verified this run) and **ran its own web search** to settle the term question. Control-theory external URLs are **preserved from branch 3** and were *not* re-fetched in synthesis (flagged in §8). External agent-loop papers cited by branch 2 (Reflexion, Self-Refine, Huang et al.) were fetched by that branch; ReAct was *not* independently fetched by any branch (flagged INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE).

**Update — source-verification pass (2026-06-28).** A targeted follow-up closed the main `INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE` items via direct web search: ReAct (arXiv:2210.03629) is now primary-source-confirmed (Yao et al., ICLR 2023); the self-correction-debate counterpoints (arXiv:2405.14092 *ProCo*; arXiv:2406.15673) were read rather than cited by-title; Self-Refine's experimental cap is **4 iterations**; and the Reflexion-paper-cited repo `github.com/noahshinn024/reflexion` now hosts an **unrelated** "Bugbounty POC" project (original code no longer at that URL). The §3 and §8 entries below reflect this pass.

## 1. Executive summary

**"Loop engineering" is a real, recently-coined industry term (mid-2026), not a settled academic discipline.** It names an older pattern: designing the system that repeatedly prompts an agent to *act → observe → verify → continue* until an evidence-based stop condition fires ([Addy Osmani](https://addyosmani.com/blog/loop-engineering/); [LangChain](https://www.langchain.com/blog/the-art-of-loop-engineering); [Claude Code docs](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/how-claude-code-works); covered skeptically by [The Register, 2026-06-24](https://www.theregister.com/ai-and-ml/2026/06/24/loop-engineering-latest-ai-buzzword-still-needs-humans-in-the-loop/5261735)).

The literature converges on one hard lesson: **an iterative self-improvement loop is only as trustworthy as (a) the independence/quality of its critique signal and (b) its bounds.** Purely intrinsic self-correction can *degrade* outputs when there is no external/oracle signal ([Huang et al., arXiv:2310.01798](https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.01798)); Reflexion and Self-Refine both ship explicit termination bounds ([arXiv:2303.11366](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.11366); [arXiv:2303.17651](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.17651)).

**This repo operationalizes that lesson well.** Across three loop surfaces it consistently implements bounded termination, never-trust-the-model clamping, explicit (non-silent) caps, and — most notably — an *independent, read-only adversarial verifier* that refuses to close a goal on the model's self-declaration (`extensions/pi-goal/index.ts:36-51`, `:362-386`). The strongest design choice is pi-goal's separation of self-check (`verifying`) from independent judgment (`verifying-independent`), a direct architectural answer to Huang et al.

**Main gaps are limitations, not defects:** the context-budget cap is best-effort and silently no-ops if usage is unknown (`caps.ts:33-37`); "quiet rounds" is an empirical convergence heuristic, not a proven fixed-point criterion; and the read-list's claim that the dynamic-cadence clamp lives in `interval.ts` is **inaccurate** — it lives in `index.ts` (`:103`, `:1186-1188`).

## 2. What "loop engineering" means (and is it an established term?)

**Verdict: established as an emerging industry term (CONFIRMED via web search this run), but not a standardized academic discipline.** Define it on first use.

Evidence found:

- **Addy Osmani** frames loop engineering as *designing the system that prompts an agent* — automation, worktrees, skills, connectors, subagents, and external state. [addyosmani.com/blog/loop-engineering](https://addyosmani.com/blog/loop-engineering/)
- **LangChain** ("The Art of Loop Engineering") frames agents as tool-calling loops, expanded into verification loops and event-driven loops. [langchain.com/blog/the-art-of-loop-engineering](https://www.langchain.com/blog/the-art-of-loop-engineering)
- **Claude Code docs** describe the agentic loop as *gather context → take action → verify results → repeat until complete*. [code.claude.com/docs/en/how-claude-code-works](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/how-claude-code-works)
- **OpenAI** (agent improvement loop cookbook) notes the area is still evolving; the durable idea is connecting feedback, testing, and implementation in one loop. [developers.openai.com/.../agent_improvement_loop](https://developers.openai.com/cookbook/examples/agents_sdk/agent_improvement_loop)
- **The Register (2026-06-24)** characterizes it as a *new AI buzzword* and cautions automation still needs cost controls and humans in the loop. [theregister.com/.../loop_engineering...](https://www.theregister.com/ai-and-ml/2026/06/24/loop-engineering-latest-ai-buzzword-still-needs-humans-in-the-loop/5261735)

**Synthesized working definition (this artifact):** *loop engineering* = the discipline of **designing, bounding, and verifying** iterative/feedback loops around agentic and software systems, so a loop makes measurable progress toward a goal and **stops on evidence** (done/quiet/blocked) rather than on a timer, a self-declaration, or never. This is the umbrella framing the repo already practices under different names (`/loop`, `loop-until-done`, `/goal`).

## 3. Foundations & prior art — with citations

| Pattern | Core idea | Primary source | Verification status |
|---|---|---|---|
| **ReAct** | Interleave reasoning traces with actions/tool calls (reason → act → observe), enabling dynamic planning + external tool use. | arXiv:2210.03629 — `https://arxiv.org/abs/2210.03629` · official site `https://react-lm.github.io/` | **CONFIRMED (source-verification pass, 2026-06-28).** Yao, Zhao, Yu, Du, Shafran, Narasimhan, Cao; v3 = **ICLR 2023** camera-ready. Interleaves reasoning traces, actions, and environment observations; evaluated on HotpotQA, FEVER, ALFWorld, WebShop. |
| **Reflexion** | Inference-time loop: Actor attempts → Evaluator scores → Self-Reflection writes verbal lessons into a **bounded episodic memory (≈1–3 entries)** prepended to the next trial. No weight updates. HumanEval pass@1 91.0 vs 80.1 baseline; **no formal convergence guarantee, can hit local minima**. | arXiv:2303.11366 — abstract `https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.11366` · PDF `https://arxiv.org/pdf/2303.11366` | Fetched by branch 2. |
| **Self-Refine** | Same model: generate `y0` → **actionable, localized** critique → refine; stop at a **fixed iteration limit** or a task-specific stop signal; history retained. Generic/absent feedback performs worse. | arXiv:2303.17651 — `https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.17651` · `https://selfrefine.info/` · code `https://github.com/madaan/self-refine` | Fetched by branch 2. |
| **Self-correction is unreliable (counter-evidence)** | Intrinsic self-correction often depends on oracle/external feedback; remove it and models (GPT-3.5/4/4-Turbo, Llama-2) **fail to improve and sometimes get worse**, flipping correct answers to wrong. Multi-round critique should be benchmarked against equal-cost self-consistency. | Huang et al., arXiv:2310.01798 (ICLR 2024) — `https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.01798` | Fetched by branch 2; **counterpoints read (2026-06-28).** (i) arXiv:2405.14092 (Wu et al., EMNLP 2024, *ProCo* — "…Key Condition Verification") shows *structured* intrinsic self-correction (mask a key condition → verify → re-solve) beats plain self-correct (+6.8 EM open-domain QA, +14.1 arithmetic, +9.6 commonsense on GPT-3.5-Turbo-1106); (ii) arXiv:2406.15673 (Liu et al., "LLMs have Intrinsic Self-Correction Ability") rebuts 2310.01798 *conditionally* — gains hold at **temperature 0 with unbiased prompts**, while biased "find problems" prompts flip correct answers (limits: 4 models, 2 datasets, no error bars). Net: Huang et al. holds for *naive/biased* intrinsic loops; structured verification or unbiased/low-temp prompting can recover gains. |
| **Control / feedback theory** | Open-loop applies a command without measuring output (cannot self-correct disturbances); closed-loop measures the process variable, compares to a setpoint, drives on the error. Stability = bounded response that decays (pole locations); excessive gain → overshoot/oscillation/instability; damping governs ringing; hysteresis = deliberate switching deadband. | Åström & Murray *Feedback Systems* (MIT) `https://introcontrol.mit.edu/_static/fall21/extras/Feedback%20Systems%20Murray.pdf`; MathWorks `https://www.mathworks.com/help/mcb/gs/open-loop-and-closed-loop-control.html`; ISA `https://www.isa.org/intech-home/2023/june-2023/features/fundamentals-pid-control`; NI `https://www.ni.com/en/shop/labview/pid-theory-explained.html`; MIT pole-zero `https://web.mit.edu/2.14/www/Handouts/PoleZero.pdf` | Preserved from branch 3; **not re-fetched in synthesis** (see §8). |

The repo's own internal index already maps these papers to workflow practices, including "loops with an explicit brake … must have a stop condition: max rounds, quiet rounds, maxAgents, timeout, or budget" (`docs/research/2026-06-25-agentic-patterns-papers-workflows.md:51-52`, derived-principle 6).

## 4. Core loop-engineering principles

Synthesized from the sources above and verified against the repo (§6). Each principle pairs a literature/theory anchor with the repo mechanism.

1. **Bounded termination (no infinite loops).** Every loop needs a stop condition. Reflexion bounds memory; Self-Refine bounds iterations ([2303.11366](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.11366), [2303.17651](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.17651)). Repo: pi-goal states "Never an infinite loop" (`pi-goal/index.ts:51`).
2. **Iteration / time / budget caps (defense in depth).** Multiple independent brakes: a hard wall-clock deadline, an iteration count, and a best-effort context-budget cap (`caps.ts:28-37`; `pi-loop/index.ts:397-398`, `:466-468`), plus a watchdog "zombie backstop" above the deadline (`pi-loop/index.ts:980-1012`).
3. **Cadence clamping ("never trust the model").** The model's chosen actuator value (delay) is *saturated* to a safe band — the agent-loop analogue of actuator-limiting to avoid destabilizing gain ([NI PID](https://www.ni.com/en/shop/labview/pid-theory-explained.html)). Repo: dynamic cadence clamped to `[60,3600]s` (`pi-loop/index.ts:103`, `:1186-1188`); fixed interval clamped to `[1s,24h]` with `0s` rejected (`interval.ts:20-21`, `:33-35`).
4. **Convergence / quiet-rounds (settle-to-tolerance).** Stop when the measured "error" (new findings) stays ≈0 for K consecutive samples — a discrete settling detector, the closed-loop analogue of a damped response settling within tolerance ([MIT pole-zero](https://web.mit.edu/2.14/www/Handouts/PoleZero.pdf)). Repo: `loop-until-done` `quietRounds` default 2, `maxRounds` default 8 (`templates.ts:351-359`).
5. **Resumability.** Persisted, rehydratable loop/goal state with a *single* catch-up tick (no burst of missed wakes) (`pi-loop/index.ts:905`; `pi-goal/index.ts:865-914`).
6. **Destructive-action gating.** Autopilot (wake-triggered) turns gate clearly irreversible/high-blast-radius actions; human turns are never gated; bias = "when unsure, do not block" (`gate.ts:52-83`, `:55-56`).
7. **Progress + INDEPENDENT verification.** The decisive lesson from Huang et al.: a model judging its own reasoning is unreliable, so close loops on an *external* signal. Repo: two-stage verification — a SELF completeness check (`verifying`), then a separate read-only adversarial subagent emitting `VERDICT: PASS|FAIL` (`verifying-independent`); only an independent PASS closes the goal (`pi-goal/index.ts:36-51`, `:362-386`).
8. **No silent caps.** A loop that stops at its budget but reports "done" hides incompleteness. Repo logs `"stopped at maxRounds (not dry)"` (`templates.ts:392-394`).

## 5. Failure modes & mitigations

| Failure mode | Mechanism / source | Repo mitigation (file:line) |
|---|---|---|
| **Runaway / token-burn** | Unbounded loop on a flawed evaluator; Reflexion gives no convergence guarantee ([2303.11366](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.11366)). | Wall-clock + iteration + context caps `caps.ts:28-37`, `pi-loop/index.ts:397-398`; watchdog backstop `:980-1012`; `maxRounds` `templates.ts:352`. |
| **Oscillation / limit cycle** (self-declares done → rejected → repeats) | Marginal/sustained oscillation absent damping ([MIT pole-zero](https://web.mit.edu/2.14/www/Handouts/PoleZero.pdf)); needs a hard switch count, not better tuning. | `maxIndependentVerifications` (default 2) → `blocked` `pi-goal/index.ts:116`, `:692-693`; self-check cap `MAX_VERIFY_ATTEMPTS=3` `:104`. |
| **No-progress / livelock** | Quiet/empty rounds that never converge. | Quiet-round detector + `maxRounds` cap `templates.ts:359`, `:389-394`. |
| **Premature stop (false settling)** | A single transient-quiet round flips the state. | Requires **two** quiet rounds (deadband) before stopping `templates.ts:351`; conservative-FAIL-on-ambiguity `pi-goal/index.ts:381-382`. |
| **Overlap / reentrancy** | Concurrent wakes racing for the turn. | FIFO serialization of non-overlapping wakeups `pi-loop/index.ts:172-177`, `:437-440`; single catch-up tick `:905`; past-timestamp clamp-to-0 `time.ts:30`. |
| **Critique-gaming / reward-hacking** (actor == critic) | Intrinsic self-correction can degrade outputs ([Huang et al., 2310.01798](https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.01798)). | INDEPENDENT read-only verifier; "a claim without verifiable evidence is a FAIL" `pi-goal/index.ts:319`; verdict anchored to last non-empty line so a PASS can't be forged by echoing the prompt `:355-360`; non-zero exit + PASS treated as FAIL `:413-417`. |

## 6. Grounding in this repo

All `file:line` verified this run. **Alignment** = mechanism matches the principle; **Gap** = limitation/discrepancy.

| Principle / failure | Repo surface (file:line) | Alignment vs gap |
|---|---|---|
| Bounded termination | `pi-goal/index.ts:51` ("Never an infinite loop"); `templates.ts:359` (`while quiet<… && round<…`) | **Aligned.** |
| Wall-clock + budget caps, checked before re-arming | `caps.ts:22-37` (doc + `capExceeded`); called at `pi-loop/index.ts:402`, `:473`, `:898` | **Aligned.** Best-effort budget cap **silently no-ops** when `getContextUsage()` is undefined or `percent` null (`caps.ts:33-37`) → **partial gap** (soft sensor, not a guarantee). |
| Iteration cap (separate gate) | `pi-loop/index.ts:397-398`, `:466-468`; default `maxIterations=25` (docstring `:72`); pi-goal default 30 (`pi-goal/index.ts:88`) | **Aligned** — cap is a distinct gate from `capExceeded`, as the read-list states. |
| Cadence clamp `[60,3600]s` (dynamic) | `pi-loop/index.ts:103` (`MAX_DELAY_SECONDS=3600`), clamp `:1186-1188` | **Aligned, but read-list inaccurate:** this clamp is in `index.ts`, **not** `interval.ts`. |
| Fixed-interval clamp `[1s,24h]`, reject `0s` | `interval.ts:20-21`, `:33-35` | **Aligned.** |
| Quiet-rounds convergence | `templates.ts:351` (`quietToStop=2`), `:389` (`quiet = fresh===0 ? quiet+1 : 0`) | **Aligned**, but empirical heuristic, not proven convergence (see §7). |
| No silent caps | `templates.ts:392-394` (`"stopped at maxRounds (not dry)"`) | **Aligned.** |
| Resumability / single catch-up | `pi-loop/index.ts:905`; `pi-goal/index.ts:865-914` (rehydrate, re-run verifier on a `verifying-independent` snapshot) | **Aligned.** |
| Reentrancy / serialization | `pi-loop/index.ts:172-177`, `:437-440`; `time.ts:30` | **Aligned.** |
| Destructive-action gating (autopilot only) | `gate.ts:52-83` (allowlist), `:55-56` ("when unsure, do not block"), `isUnsafeWritePath` `:116-133` | **Aligned.** Conservative bias is deliberate; symmetric risk = over-permissiveness on novel destructive commands not in the allowlist (see §7). |
| Independent verification closes the goal | `pi-goal/index.ts:36-51`, `:362-386`, `:653-693` (state `verifying-independent`, `runIndependentVerifier`, PASS→done / FAIL-under-cap→continue / FAIL-at-cap→blocked) | **Aligned — strongest surface.** Directly answers Huang et al. |
| Conservative verdict parsing | `pi-goal/index.ts:355-360` (anchor last non-empty line), `:381-382` (no verdict → FAIL), `:413-417` (exit≠0 + PASS → FAIL) | **Aligned.** |
| State machine | `pi-goal/index.ts:7-9`, `:30`, `:54` (`pursuing → verifying → verifying-independent → done\|blocked`) | **Aligned** with the read-list description. |
| Repo's own loop principle index | `docs/research/2026-06-25-agentic-patterns-papers-workflows.md:51-52` ("Loops with an explicit brake"), `:9-12` (paper→pattern map) | **Aligned**; ReAct/Reflexion/Self-Refine mapped. ReAct independently re-verified in the 2026-06-28 source pass (Yao et al., ICLR 2023, arXiv:2210.03629). |

## 7. Gaps, limitations, and OPTIONAL observations (no diffs)

Observation-only; no code changes proposed.

**Update — observations applied (2026-06-28).** A follow-up applied the safe, surgical items below: **#3** (read-list inaccuracy) was corrected in the research workflow's grounding read-list and reviewer prompts (`.pi/workflows/drafts/loop-engineering-deep-research.js`) so a re-run no longer misattributes the `[60,3600]s` dynamic clamp to `interval.ts`; **#6** (two distinct verify caps) and **#5** (independent-verifier cost) were addressed with cross-reference comments on `MAX_VERIFY_ATTEMPTS` and `DEFAULT_MAX_INDEPENDENT_VERIFICATIONS` in `extensions/pi-goal/index.ts`. **#1** was left as-is: `caps.ts` already documents the best-effort/silent no-op in its docstring. **#2** (eval sweep of `quietRounds`/`maxRounds`) and **#4** (finite destructive-gate allowlist) remain open by design — the former needs data, the latter is a deliberate accepted asymmetry. The practical how-to derived from this investigation lives in [`docs/loop-engineering-with-extensions.md`](../loop-engineering-with-extensions.md).

1. **Best-effort budget cap is a soft sensor.** When `getContextUsage()` is unavailable, only wall-clock + iteration brakes remain (`caps.ts:33-37`). *Optional observation:* consider documenting (already partially done in the docstring) that the context cap may silently no-op, so operators don't over-rely on it.
2. **Quiet-rounds ≠ proven convergence.** `quiet===2` is a debounce/deadband, not a fixed-point/contraction guarantee; transient quiet (finders searching the wrong place) can trigger a false settle (`templates.ts:351`, `:389`). *Optional:* the precision-vs-cost tradeoff of `quietRounds`/`maxRounds` defaults appears rationale-based, not data-based — an eval sweep would convert assumption into evidence.
3. **Read-list inaccuracy (documentation gap, not a code bug).** The grounding read-list attributes the `[60,3600]s` dynamic clamp to `interval.ts`; it actually lives in `index.ts:103`,`:1186-1188`. `interval.ts` owns only the fixed `[1s,24h]` clamp. *Optional:* correct the read-list/comments for future readers.
4. **Destructive-gate allowlist is finite.** `gate.ts` enumerates known-dangerous patterns with a "when unsure, do not block" bias (`:55-56`); a novel destructive command outside the allowlist passes on autopilot. This is a deliberate accepted asymmetry, not a defect.
5. **Independent verifier cost.** Each `verifying-independent` step spawns a separate `pi -p` process (`pi-goal/index.ts:391-424`). Huang et al. caution multi-round critique should be benchmarked against cheaper baselines (e.g., self-consistency). *Optional:* a note that more verification rounds are not free.
6. **Two distinct verify caps could confuse readers.** `MAX_VERIFY_ATTEMPTS=3` (self completeness check) vs `DEFAULT_MAX_INDEPENDENT_VERIFICATIONS=2` (independent) are different gates (`pi-goal/index.ts:104`, `:116`). Correct and intentional, but worth a one-line cross-reference for maintainers.

## 8. Coverage gaps & what to verify next

**Failed/empty branches (honest accounting):**
- `research-react-observe-act` — **EMPTY in the original run; RESOLVED 2026-06-28.** ReAct (arXiv:2210.03629) is now primary-source-confirmed: Yao, Zhao, Yu, Du, Shafran, Narasimhan, Cao; v3 = **ICLR 2023** camera-ready; interleaves reasoning traces, actions, and environment observations; evaluated on HotpotQA, FEVER, ALFWorld, WebShop ([abstract](https://arxiv.org/abs/2210.03629), [official site](https://react-lm.github.io/)).
- `research-bounding-failure-modes-term` — **EMPTY.** Synthesizer backfilled the term question via its own web search (§2, 5 sources). *Verify next:* a dedicated failure-mode taxonomy pass for parity with the other branches.
- `research-repo-grounding` — **EMPTY.** Synthesizer backfilled by reading every read-list file directly (all `file:line` here verified this run).

**Other items to verify:**
- **Control-theory URLs are preserved from branch 3 and were NOT re-fetched in synthesis** (synthesis web budget was spent on the term question). Treat F1–F7 mappings as *interpretation grounded in branch-3 citations*, pending an independent re-fetch of Åström & Murray / MathWorks / ISA / NI / MIT.
- **No peer-reviewed control-theoretic formalization of LLM loops was found.** The "quiet rounds = settling/deadband" and "clamp = actuator saturation" mappings are **analogies (INTERPRETATION)**, not proven contraction/fixed-point results. *Verify next:* search for work modeling iterative refinement as a contraction mapping with a convergence bound.
- **Self-correction debate — counterpoints now read (RESOLVED 2026-06-28).** Huang et al. ([2310.01798](https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.01798)) holds for *naive/biased* intrinsic loops. [arXiv:2405.14092](https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.14092) (Wu et al., EMNLP 2024 — *ProCo*) shows structured intrinsic self-correction (mask a key condition → verify → re-solve) beats plain self-correct (+6.8 EM QA / +14.1 arithmetic / +9.6 commonsense, GPT-3.5-Turbo-1106); [arXiv:2406.15673](https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.15673) (Liu et al.) reports intrinsic self-correction works at **temperature 0 with unbiased prompts**, while biased "find problems" prompts flip correct answers (limits: 4 models, 2 datasets, no error bars). Takeaway: bound the loop *and* keep the critique signal independent/unbiased — exactly what pi-goal's external verifier enforces.
- **Self-Refine iteration cap & Reflexion repo (RESOLVED 2026-06-28).** Self-Refine runs to a task stop signal **capped at 4 iterations** in the paper's experiments (§3.1; Fig 4 shows diminishing returns after ~3 iterations) ([arXiv:2303.17651](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2303.17651)). ⚠️ The Reflexion-paper-cited repo `https://github.com/noahshinn024/reflexion` **no longer hosts the original code** — it currently shows an unrelated "Bugbounty POC" project (~2 commits); treat that URL as a stale or repurposed citation and prefer the paper PDF ([arXiv:2303.11366](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2303.11366)).
